---
title: "QLoRA with torchao"
description: "Quantize the base model with torchao instead of bitsandbytes for compile-friendly, FSDP2-native LoRA/QLoRA."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## Background

`bitsandbytes` is the historical backend for QLoRA in Axolotl. It works well in
single-GPU and FSDP1 setups, but its 4-bit kernels graph-break under
`torch.compile` and its `Params4bit` storage needs custom patches to cooperate
with FSDP2.

[torchao](https://github.com/pytorch/ao) provides tensor-subclass quantization
(`AffineQuantizedTensor`, `NF4Tensor`) that:

- compiles cleanly with `torch.compile` (no graph breaks for INT4/INT8
  weight-only quant);
- ships native FSDP2 support via DTensor;
- shares its codebase with QAT / PTQ, so the same dtype enum (`int4`, `int8`,
  `nf4`) flows across feature.

Axolotl exposes both backends behind a single `peft:` block.

## Usage

Set `peft.backend: torchao` and pick a `weight_dtype`. The adapter type is
auto-detected from the dtype — `int4` / `nf4` imply QLoRA, `int8` stays as a
weight-only-quantized LoRA.

```yaml
adapter: lora            # auto-promoted to qlora for int4 / nf4
peft:
  backend: torchao
  weight_dtype: int4     # one of: int4, nf4, int8
  # group_size: 128      # defaults: int4/int8 → 128, nf4 → 64
```

| `weight_dtype` | Effective adapter | torchao quant config             |
|----------------|-------------------|----------------------------------|
| `int4`         | `qlora`           | `int4_weight_only`               |
| `nf4`          | `qlora`           | `NF4WeightOnlyConfig`            |
| `int8`         | `lora`            | `int8_weight_only`               |

The equivalent bnb shorthand uses the same block:

```yaml
adapter: lora
peft:
  backend: bnb
  weight_dtype: nf4      # qlora + load_in_4bit
```

```yaml
adapter: lora
peft:
  backend: bnb
  weight_dtype: int8     # lora + load_in_8bit
```

Old-style configs (`adapter: qlora` + `load_in_4bit: true`) keep working and
default to the bnb backend.

## Constraints

- **DoRA** works with torchao (the kernels dequantize through the unified
  `dequantize_weight` helper). Other PEFT extras follow standard LoRA rules.
- **Merging adapters** with `axolotl merge-lora` requires
  `merge_method: legacy`. The memory-efficient merger simulates bnb's NF4
  quantization and does not yet understand torchao tensor subclasses.
- **PEFT's `TorchaoLoraLinear`** only supports INT8. Axolotl patches
  `dispatch_torchao` so INT4 / NF4 weights fall back to standard `Linear` LoRA
  layers (the kernels dequantize the base weight on access).
- `load_in_4bit` and `load_in_8bit` are bnb-only. They cannot be combined with
  `peft.backend: torchao` — the validator will reject the config.
- `fp8`, `nvfp4`, and `mxfp4` are routed through the dedicated
  [QAT](qat.qmd) / [PTQ](quantize.qmd) flows, not the `peft.backend`
  shorthand.

## Example config

See [`examples/llama-3/qlora-torchao.yaml`](https://github.com/axolotl-ai-cloud/axolotl/blob/main/examples/llama-3/qlora-torchao.yaml).

## FSDP2

torchao tensor subclasses are first-class DTensor citizens, so the bnb-specific
FSDP2 patches (sharded-init shims, `Linear8bitLt` save patch) are skipped
automatically when `peft.backend: torchao` is in use.

```yaml
adapter: lora
peft:
  backend: torchao
  weight_dtype: nf4

fsdp_version: 2
fsdp_config:
  fsdp_offload_params: false
  fsdp_cpu_ram_efficient_loading: true
  fsdp_sharding_strategy: FULL_SHARD
  fsdp_state_dict_type: SHARDED_STATE_DICT
  fsdp_auto_wrap_policy: TRANSFORMER_BASED_WRAP
```
